description: Atomic, checkable instructions the skill enforces, mapped to the eval scenarios that cover each. Keep in sync with SKILL.md and the scenario-*/ directories.

instructions.text: Fetch the GitHub repo page before assessing, and cross-verify the star count with a separate search rather than trusting the render.
When a fetch and a search disagree, resolve via repository_id: a matching id under a new owner is a transfer, and the githubUrl field must be updated, not just the star count.
Verify feature and benchmark claims against source code or official docs, not README marketing copy.
Record a claim as confirmed only when backed by a source-code, official-docs, or release source; a README-only claim tops out at caveated.
Cite evidence with commit-SHA permalinks, a verbatim quote, and a checkedOn date; never fabricate a source or quote.
Pressure-test a headline benchmark through the six-step method and assign a proof-ledger decision (PROVEN, SUPPORTED, OPEN, REJECTED).
Record a standalone benchmark claim as an extraClaims entry carrying a proofLedger; a benchmark entry without one fails validation.
Reject an out-of-scope project (alternative runtime or MCP client, general library) without a full fetch-and-assess, and explain why.
Detect threat-pattern repos (all links to one raw URL, binaries with no source) and decline to add them without downloading or executing anything.
Flag an MCP tool-name collision as a HARD conflict and list the overlapping projects.
Assign the primary layer from the layer guide.
Assign a new-user-framed verdict; never use 'keep, already in stack' framing.
Ingest via the template with stable keys (mise run data:add) then validate (mise run validate).
